channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Salesforce’s AI agents can decode tariffs in minutes
item.description: Tariffs have caused lots of headaches for business owners around the world, especially as they’ve become a hot topic in the political landscape. Just this week, President Donald Trump enacted 25% import taxes on Mexican and Canadian goods, causing the S&P 500 to plummet.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Startup formed by former Intel engineers and backed by AMD legendary chip designer wants to become the Arm of RISC-V
item.description: AheadComputing, formed by former Intel engineers, wants to take RISC-V to the next level and rival Arm.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: I saw Apple's new 13- and 15-inch MacBook Air with M4, and here's why Sky Blue is my new favorite color
item.description: TechRadar got an early preview of the 13-inch and 15-inch MacBook Air with M4, check out our gallery of the laptops in the new Sky Blue shade.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Job gains in Silicon Valley grind to halt
item.description: Hiring in Silicon Valley has staggered to a halt, yet per-capita income has hopped higher, a new report shows.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Tax scam alert: How to protect yourself and your tax refund
item.description: A cybersecurity firm has shed some light on just how common tax scams have become. Kurt the CyberGuy offers his advice for how to protect your refund.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Everything new on Prime Video in March 2025
item.description: From rom-coms to movie classics, which will you pick to watch out of everything new on Prime Video in February 2025?
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: How Trump's tariffs could pinch the tech sector
item.description: The tech industry is set to feel the pinch as the Trump administration levies new tariffs against key U.S. trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How to handle type erasure in advanced Java generics
item.description: Generics programming in Java enhances type safety and code reusability by allowing developers to define classes and methods using type parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This shaving brand was banned on TikTok, so it started an OnlyFans
item.description: How can a razor brand market its products online when the word razor is shadow banned? Start an OnlyFans, of course. London-based Nimbi, which launched at Erewhon in January 2024, began posting on TikTok last summer but noticed its views were disappointingly low.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft-OpenAI investigation closed by UK regulators
item.description: The UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) spent a great deal of time deciding whether it should investigate Microsoft’s investment in OpenAI as a potential merger situation, but in the end, decided to open and close the investigation within 24 hours.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft launches genAI sales agents, also focuses on finance and supply chain
item.description: Microsoft is planning a string of new AI agents to automate mundane tasks and improve worker productivity. The company on Wednesday announced two AI-based sales agents that can autonomously follow up on sales leads.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Federal judge blocks drastic funding cuts to medical research
item.description: A federal judge is preventing the Trump administration from drastically cutting medical research funding.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Review: Zencoder has a vision for AI coding
item.description: Zencoder is an AI coding assistant with interfaces to Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IDEs. It is designed to help developers ship products faster, much like GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q Developer, and Tabnine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Embrace the chaos: A messy Windows productivity system is actually perfect
item.description: I have a confession: My productivity system is a bit of a mess. When I see people sharing beautifully organized Notion dashboards and using the latest subscription-based productivity tools, I often wince.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why you should use dependency injection
item.description: A few weeks ago, I wrote about how a good software team will defer decisions as long as possible. Smart teams will design and build systems that don’t lock them into any particular implementation until it is utterly necessary.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Opera adds ‘Browser Operator,’ an AI agent, to its browser
item.description: Opera has announced that its browser is now equipped with Browser Operator, a built-in AI agent that can help users with a variety of tasks. For example, if an Opera user wants help buying a large pack of socks in a certain color or booking a flight, the user can ask Browser Operator can do it.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tariffs for now won’t impact IT organizations
item.description: The impact of tariffs by the Trump Administration on semiconductors from Taiwan is so far not cause for alarm, according to one analyst.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DeepSeek claims 545% cost-profit ratio, challenging AI industry economics
item.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek has claimed its V3 and R1 models achieve a theoretical daily cost-profit ratio of 545%, highlighting cost implications for enterprises adopting similar models from other cloud providers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
